author: gpanakkal
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Leverage spaced repetition to incrementally build understanding and retain knowledge.

manifest.author: Gautam Panakkal
manifest.description: Leverage spaced repetition to incrementally build understanding and retain knowledge.
manifest.name: Incremental Reading

name: Incremental Reading

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixed a bug intermittently causing note renames to not be synced to the database
- Fixed a bug causing the cursor and scroll positions to reset during review when typing above a certain speed
- Fixed a bug sometimes causing changes to the database file to not be synced to the in-memory database
- Fixed a bug causing review lapses to be double-counted
- Fixed a bug causing incorrect time conversion to UTC
- Fixed various minor edge case bugs

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fixes:
- Action bars no longer appear inside focused table cells in articles
- Table cells no longer expand when focused in review
- Plugin warns user and unloads if it's out of date compared to the database file
- Cards no longer appear at the very top of the screen on mobile

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Combined "mark as reviewed" and "show answer" into a single hotkey
- Clicking into the priority field and leaving it without changing priority will no longer trigger a notification that priority was set
- Fixed a bug preventing fixed review intervals longer than 7 days
- Revised card grading hotkey names for clarity

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fixed a bug causing the review leaf to show "no items due for review" if the note for the next item in the queue was not found (e.g., if it was previously deleted).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New Features
- Added fixed-interval scheduling as an alternative to priority scheduling for articles. This can be toggled freely from the action bar or the **Manage item scheduling** command.
- Previously hard-coded review key binds have been replaced with customizable hotkeys, and many more have been added.
- Added a toggle on the action bar to review all items or only cards. This resets when the leaf is closed.
- Added settings to customize default priority, skip import confirmation, and adjust when a new review day begins
- Added a command to create an empty article. Useful for capturing thoughts or importing from the clipboard.
- Added action bar buttons for making snippets and cards while in review
- Action bar can now be scrolled (especially useful on mobile)
- Enabled heading/indent folding and line numbers in review
- Shows a warning notice on launch if Obsidian Sync is configured incorrectly

## Improvements and fixes
- Action bar button labels have been mostly replaced with icons
- The action bar and snippet highlights now appear in reading view
- When making cards from list items, the list formatting (including the checkbox, if there is one) will no longer be included in the card
- Fixed an issue where highlight styling would sometimes make rendered MathJax unreadable
- Clicking a snippet highlight now opens it in the same leaf
- Fixed some cases where the review leaf would fall out of sync with note content when the note was changed from another leaf
- Changes to the database file (e.g., via Sync) are now reflected immediately in review
- Newly imported articles will be immediately opened in review if the review leaf is open
- The review tab title now shows the current item's name
- Mobile header buttons now appear in review
- Lots of other UI improvements and fixes, especially for mobile

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Added a setting to disable the review UI hotkey for leaving edit mode for Vim compatibility

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fixed a bug causing a note to save and load repeatedly when editing it in the review interface

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixed a bug related to setting priorities
- Fixed action bar position and review UI displayed height on mobile

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Changed "Continue" placeholder keybind to Alt + C
- Fixed a bug where the displayed priority value would not update when proceeding to the next item in review

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adds experimental hotkeys for review actions such as grading, skipping, and dismissing. 

Hover over buttons to see their hotkeys, which are currently hardcoded.
> [!TIP]
> These hotkeys don't work when editing text; press `Escape` to leave editing mode.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Snippets now use a highlighting system instead of Obsidian embeds, preserving the source text despite revisions to the snippet
- Users can now un-dismiss items or open them in review outside of schedule by opening the note directly and using the new action bar
- Fixed various state bugs and mobile compatibility issues

releases.author: gpanakkal
releases.description:
New features:
- experimental mobile support
- file and editor context menu entries to import articles
releases.name: v0.3.1-alpha.1

releases.author: gpanakkal
releases.description:
Features:
- Obsidian sync compatibility. Make sure to enable "sync all other file types".
- Creation timestamps are now stored in frontmatter instead of file titles
- The delimiters around flash card answers were made more unique to avoid conflicting with other uses of `{{` and `}}`

Fixes:
- Newly created cards are due the next day as intended, instead of immediately
- The plugin will now unload if it fails to read the database from disk
- Eliminated dependency on Node for future mobile compatibility
releases.name: v0.3.0

releases.description:
- Add support for articles
- Add Markdown article imports
